response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 16+ years of engineering experience and demonstrable distributed systems depth from high-throughput environments, but lacks any meaningful exposure to the core competencies this role demands: experimentation platforms, A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging, telemetry infrastructure, and growth measurement. The resume shows genuine breadth across languages and domains—including recent AI agent tooling—but the total absence of experimentation and growth-specific work is a disqualifying gap for an IC6 role expected to architect and own these systems cross-team from day one.
strengths: 16+ years of diverse engineering experience spanning distributed systems, embedded, web, mobile, and AI agent infrastructure
Demonstrated high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency DLang services
Recent hands-on AI/agentic tooling work at re:cinq with MCP frameworks signals adaptability to the AI-accelerated development context
concerns: No evidence of experimentation platform experience: no A/B testing, feature flagging, rollout management, or statistical analysis of experiments mentioned anywhere
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure work visible in the resume, which is a foundational requirement for this growth and measurement-focused role

No demonstrated Principal-level cross-team technical leadership; most roles reflect individual contributor or small-team ownership without the multi-workstream, stakeholder-alignment scope IC6 requires

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or feature-flagging domain experience
No telemetry or growth metrics infrastructure work
